pr.title: rename unknown dsp func from pik1 map
pr.author: EpochFlame

summary: Renamed the placeholder Dolphin DSP function DSPSomething to DSPCancelTask in dsp.c and dsp.h, and updated DSPAssertTask to call the renamed function. The stated evidence was a Pikmin 1 map snippet showing Dolphin DSP symbols where DSPCancelTask appears as an UNUSED function in the same dsp.a/dsp.c symbol group.
what_changed: In src/dolphin/dsp/dsp.c, removed the comments labeling the function as an unknown DSP command similar to DSPReset/DSPHalt and renamed void DSPSomething(void) to void DSPCancelTask(void).
In DSPAssertTask, replaced the call to DSPSomething() with DSPCancelTask().
In src/dolphin/dsp/dsp.h, updated the function prototype from void DSPSomething(void); to void DSPCancelTask(void);
smart_moves: Used an external first-party-adjacent map source, Pikmin 1's dsp.a/dsp.c symbol list, to replace an unknown placeholder name with a plausible SDK/Dolphin symbol name.
Kept the patch tightly scoped to a pure rename plus call-site/prototype synchronization, reducing risk to matching behavior.
Preserved the function signature and implementation while improving symbol accuracy, suggesting the goal was naming correctness rather than behavioral change.
decomp_lessons: When an unknown Dolphin SDK function has matching placement and neighboring symbols in another GameCube title's map file, that map can provide strong naming evidence even if the function is UNUSED there.
Placeholder names like DSPSomething should be retired when external map evidence identifies the SDK-style symbol name.
Header prototypes must be updated together with source definitions and call sites during symbol renames to avoid stale declarations.
Neighboring known symbols in a map, such as DSPAddTask and DSPAssertTask around DSPCancelTask, can help validate an inferred name for an otherwise unknown function.
naming_conventions: Use Dolphin DSP SDK-style PascalCase names with DSP prefix, e.g. DSPCancelTask, DSPAddTask, DSPAssertTask.
Avoid vague placeholder names such as DSPSomething once a map-supported name is available.
The inferred name matches the task-oriented DSP function family: DSPAddTask, DSPCancelTask, DSPAssertTask.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Cross-reference external map files from related games to identify unknown functions by symbol order, object/library grouping, and nearby known function names.

The PR cites a Pikmin 1 map snippet for dsp.a dsp.c containing DSPCheckMailToDSP, DSPCheckMailFromDSP, DSPReadMailFromDSP, DSPSendMailToDSP, DSPReset, DSPHalt, DSPAddTask, DSPCancelTask, and DSPAssertTask.
No assembly-level rewrite or matching trick is shown; the diff is a source/header rename only.
review_feedback: One issue comment supplied the relevant Pikmin 1 map snippet as evidence for the rename.
There were no inline review comments in the provided context.
A review response of 'Looks'/'agrees' is suggested by search terms, but the provided excerpt does not include detailed reviewer reasoning.
follow_up_queries: Find other remaining DSPSomething or unknown DSP placeholders that might be resolved from Pikmin 1 or other Dolphin SDK map files.
Search for Dolphin SDK DSP function names across related GameCube maps to validate unresolved dsp.c symbols.
Check whether DSPCancelTask's implementation matches known SDK behavior from other decompilation projects or map-backed sources.
